title: logevent.v1
description: The structured log envelope every control-plane service emits — one JSON object per log line, carrying a DISTRIBUTED trace_id so a single request can be followed across services, and an `audience` split (user-facing vs system/operator). This is the SHARED SSOT for observability: each service ships its own ~20-line emitter conforming to THIS contract (the contract is the seam, not shared code).

Level.description: Syslog-style severity. `debug`/`info` are normal flow; `warning`/`error`/`critical` mark trouble.

Audience.description: Who the event is FOR. `user` — surfaced to the end user / their account timeline (a meeting joined, a transcript ready). `system` — operator/debug only (proxy hops, retries, internal state).

LogEvent.description: One structured log line. `trace_id` is the correlation key threaded edge→downstream via X-Trace-Id; every line emitted while serving one request carries the SAME trace_id.

ts.description: RFC3339 UTC timestamp the line was emitted.

service.description: Emitting service, e.g. gateway | meeting-api | runtime.

trace_id.description: The distributed correlation id. Minted at the edge if the request arrives without X-Trace-Id, then forwarded downstream verbatim so every hop shares it.

span.description: Optional per-hop/operation id within the trace (e.g. the route handler or unit of work).

user_id.description: The end user the request acts for, when known (post-auth).

meeting_id.description: The meeting/session the event concerns, when applicable.

event.description: A stable, low-cardinality event name (snake_case), e.g. request_received | downstream_forwarded | bot_join_failed.

fields.description: Optional structured context for this event (method, path, status, attempt, error, …). Free-form keys; values are JSON.
